title: Spectrally diverse laser-based near-eye display
abstract: A near-eye display device comprises a pupil-expansion optic, first and second lasers, a drive circuit coupled operatively to the first and second lasers, a spatial light modulator (SLM), and a computer. The first laser is configured to emit in a first wavelength band, and the second laser is configured to emit in a second wavelength band spectrally distinct from the first wavelength band but of the same primary color as the first wavelength band. The SLM is configured to receive emission from the first and second lasers and to direct the emission in spatially modulated form to the pupil-expansion optic. The computer is configured to parse a digital image, trigger the emission from the first and second lasers, and control the SLM such that the spatially modulated form of the emission projects an optical image corresponding to the digital image.
claims: Claims 1. A near-eye display device comprising: a pupil-expansion optic; a first laser configured to emit in a first wavelength band; a second laser configured to emit in a second wavelength band spectrally distinct from the first wavelength band but of the same primary color as the first wavelength band; a drive circuit coupled operatively to the first and second lasers; a spatial light modulator (SLM) having a matrix of electronically controllable pixel elements, the SLM being configured to receive emission from the first and second lasers and to direct the emission in spatially modulated form to the pupil-expansion optic; and coupled operatively to the drive circuit and to the SLM, a computer configured to: parse a digital image, trigger the emission from the first and second lasers by causing the drive circuit to drive a first current through the first laser and a second current through the second laser, and control the matrix of pixel elements such that the spatially modulated form of the emission projects an optical image corresponding to the digital image. 2. The near-eye display device of claim 1 wherein a peak wavelength of the first wavelength band exceeds the peak wavelength of the second wavelength band by three nanometers or more. 3. The near-eye display device of claim 1 wherein each of the first and second lasers is a diode laser. 4. The near-eye display device of claim 3 wherein each of the first and second lasers is an edge-emitting diode laser. 5. The near-eye display device of claim 1 wherein the first and second lasers differ in cavity length, in accordance with a specification. 6. The near-eye display device of claim 5 wherein a cavity length of the first laser exceeds the cavity length of the second laser by five percent or more. 7. The near-eye display device of claim 5 wherein the near-eye display device admits of a plurality of optical path lengths from the first or second laser through the pupil-expansion optic, wherein the cavity lengths of the first and second lasers correspond, respectively, to first and second longitudinal mode spacings, and wherein the cavity lengths are selected to avoid coincidence between the first and second mode spacings and the plurality of optical path lengths. 8. The near-eye display device of claim 7 wherein coincidence between the first longitudinal mode spacing and the plurality of optical path lengths gives rise to an interference fringe, and wherein the cavity length of the second laser is selected to reduce contrast of the interference fringe. 9. The near-eye display device of claim 1 wherein the pupil-expansion optic includes an optical waveguide supporting one or more diffractive optical elements. 10. The near-eye display device of claim 1 wherein the SLM is a reflective SLM. 11. The near-eye display device of claim 1 wherein the SLM is a liquid-crystal-on-silicon SLM. 12. The near-eye display device of claim 1 wherein the digital image is one of a plurality of component digital images parsed by the computer, each associated with a corresponding primary color, and wherein the computer is further configured to: coordinately control the matrix of pixel elements and the drive circuit in a time-multiplexed manner to provide field-sequential color display. 13. The near-eye display device of claim 1 comprising a beam combiner configured to geometrically combine emission from the first and second lasers into a collimated beam. 14. The near-eye display device of claim 13 wherein the beam combiner includes one or more collimating optics arranged in series with a diffuser, and wherein the diffuser and each of the one or more collimating optics are configured to receive the emission. 15. The near-eye display device of claim 13 wherein a cavity of the first laser and the cavity of the second laser lie in a plane, and wherein the beam combiner is configured to redirect emission of the first and second lasers out of the plane.
